title: C-root Jazz/Funk Chord-Scale Vocabulary
description: Practical C-root jazz/funk chord symbols with chord tones and ordered scale choices. Designed as seed data for an app that displays a chord and compatible scales.
importantNotes: Scale choices are ordered by practical default, not by absolute theory law.
Chord-scale choice depends on musical function, key center, and voice-leading context.
Chord notes list chord tones/extensions, not a required voicing.
Enharmonic spelling favors chord function: #9 may appear as D# even though it sounds like Eb.
This file emphasizes common and musically useful jazz/funk chord-scale vocabulary, not every theoretically possible pitch-class combination.

recommendedAppFunctions.name: listChords
recommendedAppFunctions.description: Display all C-root chord symbols with filtering by family, quality, tag, and commonness.

recommendedAppFunctions.name: showChord
recommendedAppFunctions.description: Given a chord id or symbol, display chord formula, chord notes, aliases, family, and voicing hints.

recommendedAppFunctions.name: showScaleChoicesForChord
recommendedAppFunctions.description: For a selected chord, display ordered scale choices with scale formulas, notes, semitones, role, and reason.

recommendedAppFunctions.name: filterByColor
recommendedAppFunctions.description: Filter chords by tonal color such as lydian, altered, diminished, whole-tone, sus, funk, blues, or tonic minor.

recommendedAppFunctions.name: sortByCommonness
recommendedAppFunctions.description: Show beginner/common chords first while keeping rare advanced chords available.

C_lydian.name: C Lydian
C_lydian.use: Bright major sound; common jazz choice for maj7(#11) colors.

C_lydian_augmented.name: C Lydian Augmented
C_lydian_augmented.use: Major seventh with #5 and #11 color; melodic-minor mode 3 sound.

C_major_pentatonic.name: C Major Pentatonic
C_major_pentatonic.use: Open major/funk color; useful over C6, C6/9, Cmaj, and some sus sounds.

C_dorian.name: C Dorian
C_dorian.use: Default jazz minor-seventh sound, especially m9/m11/m13.

C_phrygian.name: C Phrygian
C_phrygian.use: Minor sound with b9; useful for m7(b9), modal funk, dark vamps.

C_melodic_minor.name: C Melodic Minor / jazz minor
C_melodic_minor.use: Minor-major, minor 6, and sophisticated tonic minor colors.

C_harmonic_minor.name: C Harmonic Minor
C_harmonic_minor.use: Minor-major with b13; darker classical/minor-key dominant gravity.

C_minor_pentatonic.name: C Minor Pentatonic
C_minor_pentatonic.use: Funk/blues minor color over minor, sus, and dominant vamps.

C_blues.name: C Blues Scale
C_blues.use: Blue-note vocabulary over C7, Cm7, and funk vamps.

C_mixolydian.name: C Mixolydian
C_mixolydian.use: Default unaltered dominant scale: C7, C9, C13.

C_bebop_dominant.name: C Bebop Dominant
C_bebop_dominant.use: Dominant bebop vocabulary; adds natural 7 as a passing tone.

C_mixolydian_blues.name: C Mixolydian Blues
C_mixolydian_blues.use: Funk/R&B dominant color with both minor and major third.

C_lydian_dominant.name: C Lydian Dominant / C Mixolydian #11
C_lydian_dominant.use: Dominant #11 sound; common for 7#11, 9#11, 13#11 and tritone-sub colors.

C_altered.name: C Altered / C Super Locrian
C_altered.use: Maximum altered dominant tension: b9, #9, b5/#11, #5/b13.

C_half_whole_diminished.name: C Half-Whole Diminished
C_half_whole_diminished.use: Dominant diminished sound; strong for 13(b9), 7(b9), 7(#9), and diminished-dominant colors.

C_phrygian_dominant.name: C Phrygian Dominant / Mixolydian b9 b13
C_phrygian_dominant.use: Dominant b9 b13, common as V of minor or darker dominant color.

C_locrian_natural_2.name: C Locrian Natural 2
C_locrian_natural_2.use: Half-diminished with natural 9; common jazz choice for m7b5/ø9.

C_dominant_sus.name: C Dominant Sus / Mixolydian Sus
C_dominant_sus.use: Dominant sus sound without the major 3rd; useful over 7sus4, 9sus4, 13sus4.

C_augmented.name: C Augmented Scale
C_augmented.use: Symmetric augmented major color; useful for maj7#5 and augmented triad colors.

scaleChoices.reason: Common modern/jazz tonic-major color; #11 avoids the natural 11 clash with 3.
Works well for maj9 and leaves room for #11 color.
Maj13 often implies 9 and 13; Lydian gives a jazz #11 option.
The chord tones are the C major pentatonic scale.
The full C Lydian stack can be heard as Cmaj13#11.
Works as a compact augmented-major color.

chords.quality: major seventh sharp five sharp eleven

scaleChoices.reason: Default minor-seventh sound in many jazz contexts.
Useful when a natural 7 is acceptable as color or passing tone.
Contains the full m11 sound with natural 13 available.
Dorian is the source sound for m13 with natural 13.
Minor with natural 6 and major 7 color available.
Contains the m6/9 chord and supports tonic-minor color.
Works for a less leading-tone minor sound.
Contains b9 and 11 in a minor context.
Natural 9 is a common jazz color for half-diminished chords.
Contains 9 and 11 over the half-diminished shell.
Adds passing major 7 for linear phrasing.
Contains 11, but natural 11 can rub against the major 3; often voiced as sus.
Use when the 3rd is omitted or delayed.

chords.caveat: In practice, C11 is often treated or voiced like C9sus4 rather than a full 3+11 chord.

scaleChoices.reason: Great for riffs and quartal voicings.
Useful if a darker/b3 blues color is desired.
Adds b3 color if the sus dominant is used in a minor/funk context.
No major 3rd; b9 and sus4 imply a Phrygian/sus color.

scaleChoices.scaleName: C Phrygian Dominant / Mixolydian b9 b13
scaleChoices.reason: Use when the major 3rd is introduced as a resolution color.
Works if #5/b13 color is also desired.
Use when b9/#9/#5 tensions are also implied.
Use when b9/#9 tensions are included.

scaleChoices.scaleName: C Phrygian Dominant / Mixolydian b9 b13
scaleChoices.reason: Dominant b9 b13 sound, especially resolving to minor.
Use when #9 or #5/b13 colors are also desired.
Equivalent blue b3/#9 color against major 3.

scaleChoices.scaleName: C Phrygian Dominant / Mixolydian b9 b13

scaleChoices.reason: Use when #9/b5 tension is also implied.
Mixolydian has natural 13, so use with caution.
If the 5th can be altered/omitted, altered scale supplies b13.

chords.caveat: A literal 9 plus b13 plus natural 5 is not contained in one of the most common seven-note dominant scales; voicing/context matters.

chords.quality: dominant seventh flat nine flat thirteen

scaleChoices.scaleName: C Phrygian Dominant / Mixolydian b9 b13
scaleChoices.reason: Direct b9 b13 dominant sound with natural 5.
Use if the natural 5 is omitted or #5/b13 is the intended sound.

chords.quality: dominant seventh sharp nine flat thirteen

scaleChoices.reason: Use only if the 13 is natural rather than flat.

chords.quality: dominant seventh flat nine sharp five
dominant seventh sharp nine sharp five
dominant seventh flat five flat nine

scaleChoices.reason: Supplies b9 and b5 without natural 5.

chords.quality: dominant seventh flat five sharp nine

scaleChoices.reason: Supplies #9 and b5/#11 altered dominant tones.
Directly contains b9, 13, natural 5 and b7.
Treat #9 as blues b3 over dominant 13.

chords.quality: dominant thirteenth flat nine sharp eleven
dominant seventh sharp eleven sharp five
dominant ninth sharp eleven sharp five

scaleChoices.reason: Contains all standard altered dominant tensions: b9, #9, b5/#11, #5/b13.
Use if a major 7 / #11 color is desired.
Whole-half diminished contains the diminished triad and major 7 color.
Adds 4 and b7 for funk sus color.
